title: Solutions used for FISH Codetection
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"btfst","text":"protocol to make the solutions used in the protocol published by AWM lab for FISH Codetection","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: -1- MilliQ water directly pick up at thesource… -2- nuclease free water(Invitrogen AM9932)

40g in 900 ml H20 + 1 drop 10N NaOH Heat for dissolution (no more than 65°C) Then add 100 ml of x10 PBS Filter 0,2 µm Cool on ice for an immediate use. Could be kept at 4°C 15 days without problem or at -20°C
13,3 ml TEA (MERCK) Qsp 1 litre H2O Adjust to pH=8.0 (with conc HCl)

Hyb Buffer without formamide 2 ml - 20X SSC 10ml - E.coli tRNA 50mg/ml 200 µl - RNAse free water 4 ml - X50 Denhardt’s 4ml - Salmon Sperm DNA 10 mg/ml 2 ml Aliquote in 2ml tube. Keep aliquots at -20°C. These could be thawed and refreezed without pb!

Important to use desionized formamide of good quality. For this, formamide must be desionized in the lab, aliquoted (in 600 µl tube (500 µl/tube) or 1,5 ml tube(1 ml/tube) and aliquots kept at -80°Cand not at -20°C! If you thaw a tube you should not refreeze it. To deionize 500 ml formamide Weigh 25g resin AG501-X8 Add 500 ml formamide (pH 7). Stir for 2 h. Verify pH to 5.5 (if the pH is still 7, discard the solution completely). Filter the solution with 0,22 µm nylon filter (do not use nitrocellulose filter!) Label the tubes with the content. Freeze aliqiots to not be used immediately.

Maleic Acid: 58,04 g NaCl : 43,82 g Dilute in 850 ml H 2 0 and adjust the pH to 7,5 using 10N (30%) NaOH (for this add 90ml of NaOH then drop by drop. Note that the solution may stay turbid until the pH reach around 6,8). Then qsp 1 liter with H 2 0
For 200 ml 5x MAB : 40ml FBS heat inactivated:40 ml Blocking Reagent (Roche) 2 g Qsp 200 ml H 2 0 Make the solution and aliquote it in 15 ml tube. Could be thawed and refreezed.

- Solution 1 : Make a 10 mg/mlstock of the NHS ester in DMF (100 mg fluorescein NHS ester in 10 ml DMF) - Solution 2 : Make DMF-TEA solution (4 ml DMF (dimethylformamide) + 40 ul TEA (triethylamine)) - Solution 3 : Make Tyramide solution (35 mg tyramide in 3,5 ml DMF-TEA (ie in solution 2) To link the Fluorescein onto tyramide, the reaction is : - Mix 10 ml fluorescein-NHS in DMF (ie solution 1) + 3,43 ml tyramide solution (ie solution 3) - Incubate in dark at room temp for 2 hours - Then add 12 ml ethanol, - Make aliquots of 500µl in 600µl tube store in dark at 4 or -20°C
For 100 ml: - 7,5 g Glycine - Add 90 mlH 2 O - Adjust the pH to 2,1 - Qsp 100 ml H 2 O - Aliquot in 10 ml tube Keep aliquots at -20°C. Dilute 10x in PBS before use.
Stock: 5 ng/ml in water (this is kept in minus 20) Dilute 1:25.000 in PBST (2 µl per 50 ml), keep at 4°C
creator: Åsa Wallén-Mackenzie
